title: Budget Panel Bed
summary: A low-cost 2x4-framed panel bed with a plywood headboard and footboard insert and decorative trim — twin and full sizes, distinct from this catalog's stacked-1x8 panel bed.
description:
Where this catalog's other panel bed clads its headboard in horizontal 1x8 boards, this design frames a plywood panel inside a 2x4 rectangle and dresses the front with 1x4 decorative trim strips — a genuinely low-cost approach that gets a finished, layered look from construction lumber and a single half sheet of plywood. Build the outer 2x4 frame first with pocket holes, two screws per joint, then add the plywood panel and decorative trim pieces as their own layer on top of that frame — the trim is explicitly optional to inset for visual interest, a small styling choice with no structural consequence either way.

The plywood panel attaches from the back with pocket holes sized for its thinner 1/2" stock, plus nails and glue into the trim boards in front — two different fastening approaches on the same panel because the panel is doing two different jobs: structural infill from behind, decorative backing for the trim from the front.

**The side rail connection is the joint the plan is most careful about**, and for good reason — it's what carries the mattress and sleeper's full weight. Bolts, 3" screws, or bed brackets are all offered as legitimate choices, but the plan is explicit that this joint deserves more than a casual pocket-hole connection given the load it carries. Check your mattress manufacturer's slat spacing recommendation before finalizing slat count, and add more rather than fewer if there's any doubt — swap in 2x4 slats instead of 1x3 for a heavier mattress or sleeper.

materials.name: 1/2" hardwood or sanded plywood, 4 ft x 4 ft sheet
Self-tapping star bit screws, 2-1/2", or bed brackets

cutList.note: Twin size — cut to fit for full size.
Measure and cut to fit for full size.

cutList.material: 1/2" hardwood or sanded plywood, 4 ft x 4 ft sheet
1/2" hardwood or sanded plywood, 4 ft x 4 ft sheet

cutList.note: Cut to fit for full size.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two headboard frame legs at 48", four headboard and footboard frame rails at 35-3/4", two footboard legs at 21" and two siderail cleats at 75" from the 2x4. Cut one headboard vertical trim at 22-1/2", two headboard horizontal trim at 16-1/8" and one footboard vertical trim at 11" from the 1x4. Cut one headboard panel at 22-1/2" and one footboard panel at 11" from the 1/2" hardwood or sanded plywood, 4 ft x 4 ft sheet. Cut two siderail trim at 75" from the 1x8. Cut 14 slats at 38-3/4" from the 1x3 slats or other slat material. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to six 8 ft 2x4, one 8 ft 1x4 and two 8 ft 1x8. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.
materials: 1/2" hardwood or sanded plywood, 4 ft x 4 ft sheet

steps.title: Build the headboard frame
steps.body: Build the headboard's outer frame using a 1-1/2" pocket hole setting and 2-1/2" pocket hole screws, two screws per joint, with glue.

steps.title: Add the center trim piece
steps.body: Attach the center vertical trim piece, optionally inset slightly for visual interest, using a 3/4" pocket hole setting and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Add the remaining trim
steps.body: Add the remaining horizontal trim pieces to complete the headboard's decorative front.

steps.title: Attach the panel
steps.body: Drill 1/2" pocket holes (set for 1/2" stock) around the sides of the plywood panel, then attach from the back with 1" pocket hole screws. Also nail into the center trim boards with glue. Repeat this whole process for the footboard.
materials: 1/2" hardwood or sanded plywood, 4 ft x 4 ft sheet

steps.title: Connect the headboard and footboard
steps.body: Connect the headboard and footboard with the 2x4 siderail cleats, using bolts, 3" screws, or bed brackets — this is a main structural joint carrying the bed's weight, so make it strong.
materials: Self-tapping star bit screws, 2-1/2", or bed brackets

steps.title: Attach the siderail trim
steps.body: Attach the decorative siderail trim to the inner siderail cleats, and to the headboard and footboard for added strength.

steps.title: Add the slats and finish
steps.body: Check your mattress manufacturer's slat spacing recommendations, and add more slats than needed if in doubt — use 2x4s instead of 1x3 for heavier loads. Attach the slats to the inner side rails. Sand, fill, and finish.

unresolvedImages.alt: Budget panel bed with a plywood headboard insert and decorative trim
